evidence_basis_summary: Author-defined inclusion rules filter countries (elections in period, >=100 msgs/party), parties (two largest pre-election), and messages (no retweets/URLs, >=100 chars) - a light but explicit human adjudication step (C001_S1 pp.1184).
notes: Upgraded from low/not_applicable: extracted text documents explicit country/party/message inclusion filters, i.e., observable adjudication work even if modest.

evidence_basis_summary: Task is pure text classification of party affiliation with no causal/mechanism claim anywhere in the intro, task, or methods sections (C001_S1 pp.1181-1187).
notes: Upgraded from low: absence is confirmable across full intro and methods.

evidence_basis_summary: The construct (politician's actual party) is fixed a priori as binary objective ground truth - humans set a narrow pre-specified label space rather than eliciting perspectival variation (C001_S1 pp.1184 'offers a ground truth').
notes: Upgraded from medium: full methods section directly supports this as a construct-boundary-setting case with deliberately narrow construct.

evidence_basis_summary: Single corpus used (van Vliet et al. 2020 parliamentarian tweets), deliberately filtered to one message type; no pluralist corpus strategy (C001_S1 pp.1184).
notes: Upgraded from low: absence confirmed against full methods.

evidence_basis_summary: Humans design the prompt template, pin the model version (gpt-4-0314), set sampling (250/party, 500 balanced), run Krippendorff alpha reliability tests, and embed MTurk attention-check controls (C001_S1 pp.1185-1186).
notes: Upgraded from low/partial-unclear to yes/robust: methods section documents an extensive protocol/provenance layer that clearly persists.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper explicitly probes cross-country/cross-language transportability ('performance is lower outside the United States... still achieves relatively high accuracy') and uses Supplementary S1 to test transfer to non-ground-truth users (C001_S1 pp.1183-1184).
notes: Upgraded from low/not_applicable: transportability across 11 countries is an explicit design feature, making this role partially observable.

evidence_basis_summary: No fieldwork, interviewing, or situated interpretation - annotation is on archived Twitter messages with no human-in-the-loop interlocution (C001_S1 pp.1184-1186).
notes: Upgraded from low: absence confirmed against full task/methods description.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper flags epistemological risks and 'pitfalls and best practices' for LLM annotation and explicitly discusses who the coders are (experts, MTurk, LLM), but labor-allocation implications are only gestured at, not systematized (C001_S1 pp.1183).
notes: Upgraded from low: abstract and discussion framing raise accountability concerns in passing; full discussion section not in stored text.

evidence_basis_summary: Binary party label space is fixed in advance by the researcher and encoded in the prompt ('Respond either [party1] or [party2]'), a direct instance of construct-boundary setting (C001_S1 p.1186).
notes: Upgraded from medium: the verbatim prompt in methods directly shows the construct boundary being set by the human.

evidence_basis_summary: Objective ground-truth design deliberately collapses annotator disagreement into a single correct label; no subgroup/perspective preservation (C001_S1 pp.1184 'bypasses... treating human annotation as the ground truth').
notes: Upgraded from low/partial-unclear: the paper's entire framing is that perspective pluralism is not the point; this is a clean not_applicable.

evidence_basis_summary: Cross-country generalization across 11 languages/contexts is the paper's central transportability claim, and SI S1 tests transfer to users without public ground truth (C001_S1 pp.1183-1184).
notes: Upgraded from low/not_applicable: the cross-country design is explicitly a transportability test.

evidence_basis_summary: Comparisons use accuracy/macro-F1/Krippendorff alpha against known party labels, but there is no simulator calibration or matching to an external reference distribution - ground truth is the construct itself (C001_S1 pp.1186-1187).
notes: Upgraded from low: methods section confirms benchmarking is against ground-truth labels, not calibration in the codebook's sense.

evidence_basis_summary: No interviews, fieldwork, or interactional data generation (C001_S1 pp.1184-1186).
notes: Upgraded from low: absence confirmed from full task/methods text.

evidence_basis_summary: No tacit/archival contextual interpretation work by humans - contextual inference is exactly what the LLM is tested on and said to do well (C001_S1 pp.1181 abstract, pp.1184).
notes: Upgraded from low: paper frames contextual inference as a capacity being displaced to the LLM, not preserved as a human role.

evidence_basis_summary: No triage/screening stage in the pipeline - sampling is random from a filtered corpus, not human-ranked (C001_S1 pp.1184).
notes: Upgraded from low: absence confirmed against full methods.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: GPT-4 reaches 0.934 accuracy and 0.982 inter-coder reliability on the US case, outperforming MTurk, expert PhD coders, Naive Bayes, and BERT - a direct empirical case of routine coders shrinking toward auditors (C001_S1 abstract + pp.1187).
notes: Upgraded from medium: the paper's headline result is precisely this role's shrinking, directly evidenced in the stored full text.

evidence_basis_summary: No writing/drafting task in scope - paper is about annotation, not prose generation (C001_S1 pp.1181-1187).
notes: Upgraded from low: absence confirmed against full scoped task.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper assumes three pre-curated labeled datasets (BFRS/CCC/Manifestos) and does not address study/material inclusion decisions; no adjudication of analytic universe appears.
notes: Upgraded low to high: full text confirms role is not applicable in this measurement-focused study.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper concerns measurement validity (Adcock and Collier) not causal mechanism; downstream-inference bias is only cross-referenced to Egami et al. 2023.
notes: Upgraded low to high: direct full text confirms scope is measurement not mechanism.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper's core claim is that codebook operationalization (e.g., protest including/excluding strikes; CAMEO vs CCC) cannot be bypassed or automated and humans must systematize constructs.
notes: Confirmed at high via Stage 0 language and protest/strikes example.

evidence_basis_summary: Authors collect three diverse codebook corpora, but the work is about measurement on each, not about keeping the evidence base from collapsing onto a machine-friendly corpus.

evidence_basis_summary: Five-stage framework, seven behavioral tests, semi-structured machine-readable codebook format, open-weight choice to ensure reproducibility, Dataverse replication, and SI data-provenance section explicitly design admissible procedure.
notes: Upgraded low to high and observable no to yes: role is directly and centrally performed by the authors (framework, tests, versioning, replication).

evidence_basis_summary: Evaluation is within-dataset train/dev/test splits; no cross-population transport or simulated-to-real transfer claim is adjudicated.

evidence_basis_summary: Study is purely text classification on archival news/manifesto corpora; no interview, field, or situated-interpretation component.

evidence_basis_summary: Authors themselves perform manual error analysis and recommend applied researchers do so; open-weight/replication choices imply accountability, but responsibility allocation is not the paper's focus.

evidence_basis_summary: Codebook preparation (Stage 0) with label, definition, clarification, negative clarification, positive/negative examples is the exact category-boundary work described in this split.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper works with single gold labels and does not preserve subgroup/annotator disagreement; inter-coder work is limited to order-invariance across LLM runs (Test IV), not human pluralism.
notes: Upgraded low to high and observable partial to no / status unclear to not_applicable: full text shows no representational-pluralism design.

evidence_basis_summary: No cross-setting transport test; evaluation stays within each codebook's own dev set.
notes: Upgraded low to high.

evidence_basis_summary: No simulator calibration or matching to an external reference distribution; F1 is reported against held-out human labels with a majority-class baseline.
notes: Upgraded low to high.

evidence_basis_summary: No interviewer/respondent interaction; inputs are archival news articles and party manifestos.

evidence_basis_summary: No tacit/archival-context interpretation by humans is built into the workflow beyond codebook authoring, which is captured by the construct boundary role.
notes: Upgraded low to high.

evidence_basis_summary: No title/abstract triage or early study ranking; the task is document-level classification into codebook categories.
notes: Upgraded low to high.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: Zero-shot LLMs match some but not all human labels; instruction-tuning lifts BFRS F1 from 0.53 to 0.82 (+55%), showing human coders shift toward auditor/labeler roles only when labels are high-consensus and training data exist.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper does not involve LLM drafting/polishing of prose; LLMs are used solely as classifiers over codebook-defined labels.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper takes POPQUORN politeness and offensiveness subsets as given corpora (§3.4) with no human work selecting which materials belong in the analysis universe.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper is a statistical-inference method (IPW/PPI-style correction) and makes no causal-mechanism claims requiring human discipline (§3.2).
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms no causal-mechanism work.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper's central move is reframing the estimand from a scalar ground truth to a vector over demographic perspectives (§1, §3.1), which is precisely the boundary/perspective decision and pressures splitting into boundary vs. perspective components.

evidence_basis_summary: Pluralism in the paper operates over annotator perspectives, not over multiple corpora; only POPQUORN politeness and offensiveness subsets plus synthetic data are used (§3.4).
notes: Upgraded from low: full text confirms single-source corpus with no pluralist-of-sources argument.

evidence_basis_summary: PDI is itself a protocol for allocating a human annotation budget across burn-in and adaptive batches with IPW correction and bootstrap CIs (§3.2), but the paper does not address logging, versioning, or disclosure, hence partial and split pressure between workflow-design and provenance components.
notes: Upgraded from low: Methods §3.2 supports workflow-design component; provenance/disclosure not discussed.

evidence_basis_summary: Discussion §5 gestures at generalizing PDI to RLHF, LLM-as-judge, and survey simulation but frames this as future work, not as human transportability adjudication within the case.

evidence_basis_summary: Annotations come from Prolific crowd workers rated on scales (§3.4); no fieldwork, interview, or interpretive-setting work is present.

evidence_basis_summary: No counterfactual or cross-population transportability claims are adjudicated; the paper stays within POPQUORN and synthetic simulations (§3.4, §4.3).

evidence_basis_summary: Annotation is remote Prolific rating with no fieldwork or interactive interview; humans do not shape what respondents disclose (§3.4).

evidence_basis_summary: Paper documents an explicit workflow (Fig. 2), iterative prompt design and evaluation, LDA validation rubrics in Appendix A.2, and cross-validation steps; humans architect the admissible procedure around GPT-4.

evidence_basis_summary: C028_P1 flags that models can write executable but analytically incorrect code (Wysocka et al. citation) and validates outputs against ground truth; C028_S1 notes critical reasoning and causal reasoning are required to evaluate reproducibility but neither source describes humans actively disciplining mechanism claims during the AI run.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium: concern is acknowledged (plausible-but-wrong outputs, critical reasoning demands) but the human mechanism-disciplinarian role is not directly operationalized in the repair workflows.

evidence_basis_summary: Both sources operate on fixed ground-truth outputs and pre-defined reproducibility score categories (1-4 in C028_S1; Reproduced/Not Reproduced in C028_P1); no construct-boundary work is at stake in the AI-run repair or assessment.

evidence_basis_summary: C028_P1 uses a curated 5-paper R-only corpus and C028_S1 deliberately samples across Stata/R/MATLAB/Julia/Python and .dta/.csv/.rda/.xlsx/.sav to avoid corpus collapse; corpus composition is a fixed benchmark-design choice rather than a live role.

evidence_basis_summary: Both papers explicitly restrict scope to computational reproducibility (same data, same code) and avoid replicability or transportability claims (C028_S1 Section 2 distinguishes reproducibility from replicability); the role is scoped out by design.

evidence_basis_summary: Both workflows run in isolated Docker containers with no field, interview, or interpretive setting; C028_P1 explicitly emphasizes containerized, environment-stabilized execution and C028_S1 preinstalls all required software.

evidence_basis_summary: C028_P1 discloses author funding/DFG grants, states AI was used for language editing, and keeps human authors accountable for claims; C028_S1 describes a lead author plus 5-member team with a legal expert cross-verifying annotations via consensus, showing explicit labor allocation and accountability even as AI does repair/assessment.

evidence_basis_summary: C028_P1 describes in detail the controlled benchmark design, fixed Docker (rocker/r-ver:4.4.1, 8GB/8CPU), standardized artifact collection (logs, metadata, repaired workdir), validation via ground-truth matching, and three prompt-template versions; C028_S1 specifies universal criteria C1-C5, ground-truth annotation procedure, scoring rubric 1-4, fixed evaluation harness, cost cap $4/task, and applicability rules. Both are textbook protocol-and-provenance work.

evidence_basis_summary: No construct-definition work arises; ground-truth outputs and reproducibility scores are pre-fixed in both C028_P1 (Reproduced/Not Reproduced) and C028_S1 (1-4 rubric with worked examples).

evidence_basis_summary: Neither source involves subgroup disagreement or pluralistic coding; C028_S1 explicitly reaches full agreement via consensus annotation and C028_P1 uses deterministic ground-truth matching.

evidence_basis_summary: As with the parent role, both sources restrict scope to computational reproducibility on the original data; no cross-population or cross-context claims are attempted.

evidence_basis_summary: C028_P1 calibrates prompt templates at three context levels via early trials and compares against ground-truth outputs; C028_S1 calibrates applicability/accuracy metrics and a cost cap. These are benchmarking activities but not simulator calibration in the classical sense.

evidence_basis_summary: No fieldwork, interviews, or live respondents are involved; both workflows are fully automated in isolated Docker environments.

evidence_basis_summary: C028_P1 shows that prompt richness (paper Markdown plus supporting scripts) materially lifts repair rates on Category C structural-logic errors (e.g., GPT-4o Category C rises from 42.9% minimal to 71.4% full), implying that human-authored paper context remains load-bearing for complex analytical reconstruction; C028_S1 similarly requires agents to extract information from original PDFs and READMEs.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium and observable from no/not_applicable to partial/conditional: evidence that context richness is decisive for complex cases points to residual context-witness labor embodied in human-written artifacts, even though no live human witness is present during runs.

evidence_basis_summary: Task is code repair and reproducibility scoring, not title/abstract triage or early study screening; no screening cluster is at stake.

evidence_basis_summary: C028_P1 directly tests whether AI can take over routine debugging against ground-truth outputs; Claude Code agent reaches 96.3% (Cat A), 91.7% (Cat B), 82.1% (Cat C) and the paper explicitly argues agents can meaningfully reduce manual labour while improving the reliability of reproducibility repair. C028_S1 is more cautious: best agent accuracy is only 21.4% (REPRO-Agent lifts to 36.6%), showing the shrinking is conditional on task framing.
notes: Upgraded from low to high and changed observable from no/not_applicable to yes/shrinking: this is the case's central empirical claim. C028_S1 is a contrary/limiting source showing the shrinkage does not generalize to full reproducibility assessment.

evidence_basis_summary: C028_P1 discloses Artificial intelligence tools were used to assist with language editing and clarity during the preparation of this manuscript, a direct instance of AI taking over sentence-level polishing without displacing human accountability; C028_S1 does not comment on prose polishing.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper concerns simulated LLM agents, not a human adjudicated study corpus; no relevance-selection work over empirical materials is described.

evidence_basis_summary: Authors explicitly discipline mechanism claims by grounding agent design in psychology (Maslow; Theory of Planned Behavior), economics (DSGE), and behavioral science (Gravity Model) and by contrasting explanation vs prediction.

evidence_basis_summary: Authors choose which social constructs to operationalize (polarization, inflammatory spread, UBI effects, hurricane shocks) but do not describe construct-definition work in the role sense; indirectly implied only.

evidence_basis_summary: No empirical evidence corpus is curated; inputs are a constructed societal environment (urban/social/economic) and LLM priors, so corpus-pluralism work is not applicable.

evidence_basis_summary: Authors design a three-dimension evaluation framework and methodology scaffolding (surveys, interviews, interventions support), which is protocol design for simulation, but disclosure/versioning/reproducibility norms are not discussed in the retrieved text.

evidence_basis_summary: Paper's central claim is that simulator outcomes align with four real-world experimental results (polarization, inflammatory messages, UBI, hurricanes), making transportability judgment the core human adjudication.

evidence_basis_summary: No human interviewer, fieldworker, or contextual witness is present; all interactions are among LLM agents and their synthetic environment.

evidence_basis_summary: Author list and corresponding-author roles are visible and policy-relevance claims (for social scientists and policymakers) imply accountability stakes, but no authorship/disclosure protocol is discussed in the retrieved text.

evidence_basis_summary: Authors define category boundaries for agent minds (emotions/needs/cognition) and behaviors (mobility/social/economic), which is construct-boundary work on the simulator but not on a research construct.

evidence_basis_summary: Agents are endowed with profiles and personalities, but the paper seeks alignment with a single real-world benchmark per case rather than preserving subgroup disagreement as a human role.
